note: Day-3 offline reproduction of final-selection consistency, semantic tie-breaking, and derived-alias sanitation. Deterministic; no live LLM. Fixes are schema-generic (no DB/test hardcoding).

db56_t51.question: Show each department with the number of unused beds based on bed capacity minus current occupancy.
db56_t51.new_selected_sql: SELECT department_name, bed_capacity, current_occupancy, (bed_capacity - current_occupancy) AS unused_beds FROM departments

db55_t142.question: How many distinct programs have students advised by instructors from the same department as the program?
db55_t142.old_selected: llm_sql_direct_variant (adds redundant academic_advising join) via label tie

population_tie_break.reason: equal score; fewer unrequested population-restricting joins / unrequested output columns

db55_t142.old_selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.program_id) FROM programs p JOIN students s ON p.program_id=s.program_id JOIN instructors i ON s.advisor_instructor_id=i.instructor_id JOIN departments d ON i.department_id=d.department_id JOIN academic_advising aa ON s.student_id=aa.student_id WHERE p.department_id=i.department_id
db55_t142.new_selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.program_id) FROM programs p JOIN students s ON p.program_id=s.program_id JOIN instructors i ON s.advisor_instructor_id=i.instructor_id JOIN departments d ON i.department_id=d.department_id WHERE p.department_id=i.department_id

db56_t401.question: List doctor identifiers that appear either as primary doctors or appointment doctors.
db56_t401.old_selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT doctor_id FROM doctors UNION SELECT DISTINCT doctor_id FROM appointments
db56_t401.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT d.doctor_id FROM doctors d WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM patients p WHERE p.primary_doctor_id=d.doctor_id) OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM appointments a WHERE a.doctor_id=d.doctor_id)
db56_t401.note: Role-to-column grounding now maps 'primary doctors' -> patients.primary_doctor_id and 'appointment doctors' -> appointments.doctor_id; the all-doctors branch fails role provenance and the role-grounded candidate (72 rows) is selected. See day3_role_and_value_replay.json for full provenance detail.

db56_t403.question: List patient identifiers that appear either in appointments or billing claims

alias_scope.cross_branch_alias_reuse_sql: SELECT p.patient_id FROM appointments p UNION SELECT p.patient_id FROM billing_claims p

db56_t403.new_selected_sql: SELECT p.patient_id FROM appointments p UNION SELECT p.patient_id FROM billing_claims p

db56_t96.question: For each patient, calculate abnormal lab tests as a percentage of completed lab tests.
db56_t96.executable_sql: SELECT patient_id, SUM(CASE WHEN abnormal_flag IN ('critical','high','low') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS abnormal_count, COUNT(*) AS total_count, 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN abnormal_flag IN ('critical','high','low') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS abnormal_percentage FROM lab_tests WHERE test_status='completed' GROUP BY patient_id
db56_t96.value_grounding_note: abnormal set grounded to critical/high/low, normal excluded; denominator = completed tests; safe NULLIF division. (Live value-grounding of the abnormal_flag literal to critical/high/low is a separate extractor/value-profile concern; the alias-sanitation fix removes the no-such-column failure that previously killed every candidate.)
